title: Idempotent Consumers, Inbox Records, and External Effects · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for idempotent consumers, inbox records, and external effects and requires the smallest defensible durable inbox and duplicate-safe effect protocol with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for durable inbox and duplicate-safe effect protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata.
Record inbox identity, input fingerprint, state transition, domain effect, and completion atomically in one database transaction where possible.
Reject reuse of an operation identity with a different payload fingerprint or semantic intent and retain conflict evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Inbox Retention Replay
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz messaging team handles case 1360 in a benefit-notification consumer near its database pool limit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; keep credentials and connection URLs out of learner output; then fail the canary and prove older consumers remain compatible during rollback. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Set inbox retention from maximum redelivery, replay, audit, privacy, and disaster-recovery windows and test expired-key behavior.
